findings.description: Cover page logo/image is missing in the HTML rendering
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a large NC Coastal Recreational Fishing License logo/image centered on the page. The HTML rendering does not show this page at all as a separate cover page.

findings.description: Marine Resources Fund Balance table in PDF has detailed spreadsheet format with dollar signs and precise alignment that is split across PDF page 3 and HTML pages differently
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows a comprehensive spreadsheet-style table with Revenue/Expense rows including Agent Commission, Transaction Fee, Subtotal (Expense), and Total Net. The HTML splits the expense portion to a separate rendering on page 4 of HTML, but data appears preserved.

findings.description: For Hire Blanket table years are presented differently - PDF shows separate sub-tables per year while HTML combines into a single table with Year column
findings.evidence: PDF page 30 shows separate tables for each year (2007, 2008, 2009, etc.) with individual headers. HTML page 33 combines these into one continuous table with a 'Year' column added.

findings.description: DMF Sales Office table - the % Sold column totals row is missing percentage values in both PDF and HTML, consistent rendering
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML omit total percentages in the sales outlet table totals row, which is consistent.

findings.description: In the Marine Resources Endowment Fund table, the 'Transfers from WRC March 2006-2010' row has '5 transfers of $680,000 each' repeated across all year columns instead of being a single description spanning the row as in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Transfers from WRC March 2006-2010' with '5 transfers of $680,000 each' as a description and $3,400,000 as the total. HTML repeats '5 transfers of $680,000 each' in every fiscal year column.

findings.description: In the Marine Resources Endowment Fund table, the 'Non Capital Gifts' row shows '$ -' in FY2007-2010 and '$ -' in FY2014 columns in the HTML, but the PDF shows '$ -' only once (no value in FY2014 column specifically) and the row structure is different.
findings.evidence: PDF: Non Capital Gifts row has '$ -' once. HTML adds '$ -' in both FY2007-2010 and FY2014 columns.

findings.description: The country-by-country table shows MEXICO in 2014 with 'No. Sold' of 0 but rank of 4. The PDF shows MEXICO 2014 has 0 sold with no rank listed (rank column appears blank).
findings.evidence: PDF page 13: MEXICO 2014 shows '0' sold with rank '4'. Actually this appears to match the PDF which does show 0 and 4 - this may be a PDF data quirk rather than an HTML error.

findings.description: The document uses h1 for 'Coastal Recreational Fishing License' and h2 for 'Sales Update', which is acceptable, but then uses h3 for major section headers like 'Marine Resources Fund Balance' and 'Marine Resources Endowment Fund Balance'. The hierarchy is consistent but could be questioned since there's no h2 section between the title and h3s.
findings.evidence: h1: 'Coastal Recreational Fishing License', h2: 'Sales Update', h3: 'Marine Resources Fund Balance', h3: 'Marine Resources Endowment Fund Balance', h3: 'Projects Approved for Funding...'

findings.description: The HTML has a nested <main> element inside another <main> element, which is a structural duplication, though it doesn't affect content.

In the 'Wilmington' row of DMF Sales Office table for 2010, the PDF shows '15.53' as Wilmington's % Sold but the column header in the PDF for 2010 shows Wilmington at rank under 'No. Sold' of 331 and '% Sold' listed differently depending on reading. HTML appears to match correctly at 15.53.
findings.evidence: Verified: HTML matches PDF for Wilmington 2010: 331 sold, 15.53%.
